user_query: Read input/births.json (three people) and compute their BaZi Four Pillars charts using precise solar-term month boundaries and true solar time correction for each person’s city. For each person, produce a structured analysis that includes: (1) pillars.year/month/day/hour each with "stem", "branch", and "hidden_stems"; (2) five_elements with keys wood/fire/earth/metal/water, each having "score" (number), "percent" (string like "20%"), and "chinese"; (3) day_master_strength labeled exactly as "偏强" or "偏弱"; (4) major_luck with "direction" ("顺排" or "逆排"), integer "start_age", and a "periods" array of at least 8 entries each containing "age" (e.g., "3-12"), "years" (e.g., "1993-2002"), "stem", and "branch"; (5) relationships as a list of clash/combine/harm findings (can be empty). Write a combined JSON to output/charts.json with an array field "results" where each item includes an "id" and an "input" object echoing the original fields (date, time, city, gender), plus the computed fields (pillars, five_elements, day_master_strength, major_luck, relationships). Also write a CSV to output/summary.csv with header: id,gender,birth_local_time,city,day_master_stem,major_luck_direction,major_luck_start_age,dominant_element_en. Fill each row so that: birth_local_time = "{date} {time}" from the input; day_master_stem = the day pillar’s stem; major_luck_direction and major_luck_start_age match major_luck.direction and major_luck.start_age; dominant_element_en is the element key with the highest five_elements.score. Keep all paths relative and use only input/ and output/ as specified.
